title: COURSE FINAL: Complete mastery
description: The final exam — 105 WPM on 400+ chars of comprehensive real-world prose. Pass at 80 WPM = Platinum, 60 = Gold, 40 = Silver, 25 = Bronze.
text: Ninety-nine lessons ago, you sat down at a keyboard for the first time as a student. Today, you sit down as something else entirely: a typist. Your fingers know the keyboard the way a pianist knows a piano. Your eyes scan ahead by entire phrases. Your hands no longer translate thought into text — they have become a continuation of your thinking. Take a moment. This is the last exercise. Type smoothly, breathe steadily, and finish the way you started: with care.

tips.text: The last page. Some 420 characters of real prose at 105 a minute. Type it the way you mean it.
80 a minute earns Platinum, 60 Gold, 40 Silver, 25 Bronze. The accuracy is what's being read.

tips.text: When this page is done, so is the course. You started a student; you finish a typist. - Eleanor Hayes

tips.title: A last word
tips.text: Slow down if you have to. Accuracy is respect, and it's accuracy that sets the certificate. - Eleanor Hayes

tips.target: ahead by entire phrases. Your hands no longer translate thought into text — they have become a continuation of your thinking. Take a moment. This is the last exercise. Type smoothly, breathe steadily, and finish the way you started: with care.

character_tips.anna: The final page. Ninety-nine lessons brought you here; one paragraph closes it. Type with care.
character_tips.maxim: The end of the road. Slow down if you must, but finish the way you'd want it read.
character_tips.knopych: One paragraph left in the whole course. Keep the rhythm and let it carry you home.
character_tips.klavochka: The last lines, my dear. A slow breath, then type them cleanly. You always could. - Eleanor Hayes
